questionHtml: The scientists wish to investigate the claim that Group B gain weight faster than Group A. Test this claim at the 5% level of significance, noting which hypothesis test you are using. You may assume that the weight gain for each group is normally distributed, with the same variance, and independent from each other.
markschemeHtml:
This is a t-test of the difference of two means. Our assumptions are that the two populations are approximately normal, samples are random, and they are independent from each other.          (R1)

H0: μ1 − μ2 = 0

H1: μ1 − μ2 < 0          (A1)                

t = −2.460,          (A1)

degrees of freedom = 38          (A1)

Since the value of critical t = −1.686 we reject H0.          (A1)

Hence group B grows faster.          (R1)

[6 marks]
